title: init_db() wipes the database on every startup
evidence: app.py:36 init_db() calls db.drop_all() then db.create_all(); it runs from __main__ (app.py:151), so every `python app.py` deletes all persisted books
suggestion: Call only db.create_all() (idempotent), or guard drop_all() behind an explicit reset flag, so R7 persistence survives restarts

title: PUT author update strips before str() coercion
evidence: app.py:123 uses str(data['author'].strip()) (strip on raw value) unlike title at app.py:118 which uses str(...).strip(); a non-string author (e.g. numeric) raises AttributeError -> 500
suggestion: Use str(data['author']).strip() to match the create/title path and avoid a 500 on numeric input

title: Dead unused make_client generator in tests
evidence: test_app.py:11-27 defines make_client() (a bare generator, not a fixture) superseded by the client fixture at test_app.py:30; its db_path var (line 15) is also unused
suggestion: Delete make_client() to reduce test-file noise

title: No pinned dependency file
evidence: run dir has no requirements.txt/pyproject.toml; README.md only lists `pip install flask flask-sqlalchemy` inline
suggestion: Add requirements.txt with pinned flask and flask-sqlalchemy versions for reproducible setup

title: 12 tests provided vs 3 required
evidence: test_app.py has 12 tests (grep -c '^def test_') covering CRUD, ?author= filter, validation 400s, and 404 paths; scores.json test_coverage=0.88, defect_rate=1.0 (all pass)
